channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Thermal Master P4 review: Easily the best phone-connected thermal camera yet, but it's not perfect
item.description: The Thermal Master P4 is a thermal camera that connects to an Android phone or PC using USB.

item.title: Rednote Draws a Line Between China and the World
item.description: As the platform expands abroad, it’s taking steps to separate Chinese users from the international audiences it once brought together.

item.title: FCC challenges gender identity themes on kids TV
item.description: The federal agency calls into question entire system of TV ratings.

item.title: Instagram tests a new ‘Instants’ app for sharing disappearing photos
item.description: The app lets users share disappearing photos with their friends that can be viewed only once and remain available for 24 hours.

item.title: Microsoft is reportedly offering voluntary buyouts to up to 7 percent of its employees
item.description: Microsoft is planning to get rid of more US employees via its first voluntary buyout program, CNBC reports. The buyout program will reportedly be offered to US employees at "the senior director level and below whose years of employment and age add up to 70 or higher," and could cover up to 7 percent of the company's US workforce. With around 125,000 employees in the US as of June 2025, that could mean up to 8,750 will be offered a paid exit when Microsoft begins its program in May. That's a smaller figure than the 15,000 or so employees the company laid off in May and July of 2025, but still s...

item.title: Spotify is celebrating its 20th anniversary, and so are we — these are 5 of the platform’s landmark moments and yes, one of them involves Taylor Swift
item.description: These are 5 of Spotify's most memorable moments from the last 20 years.

item.title: The dark side of AI —shocking report reveals 11 planned AI data centers connected to OpenAI, Meta and Microsoft could produce more greenhouse gases than Morocco
item.description: New AI data centers could emit more greenhouse gases than an entire nation.

item.title: Meta will lay off 10% of workforce, company told staff today
item.description: As Meta spends heavy on AI initiatives and pushes for efficiency, 8,000 employees will be cut and 6,000 open roles shut. Meta announced to employees on Thursday that the company is laying off around 10% of the company on May 20—about 8,000 employees out of its workforce of more than 78,000 will be impacted, Bloomberg first reported.

item.title: No, Capital One didn’t bankroll an influencer’s wedding—but if you have enough followers, a big brand might sponsor yours
item.description: Wedding costs keep skyrocketing. And while there’s little relief in sight for most of us, some brides and grooms are softening the blow with sponcon. Modern brides need something old, new, borrowed, and blue. But if they’re influencers, add a sponsor to the list, too.

item.title: Sonos’ big sale on refurbished speakers is about to end
item.description: Portable audio and home theater upgrades can be very pricey, which is why we make a habit out of telling readers when there are good deals happening. With that in mind, here’s a friendly reminder that a heap of refurbished Sonos gear is steeply discounted, and will be until the company’s sale ends April 24th. […]

item.title: Meta is laying off 10 percent of its staff
item.description: Meta is planning to layoff around 10 percent of employees in May, according to a memo from the company's chief people officer, Janelle Gale, published by Bloomberg. That means approximately 8,000 people will see their jobs cut. Meta will also be closing around 6,000 open roles, according to Gale. The cuts follow Meta's significant investments […]

item.title: Meet Noscroll, an AI bot that does your doomscrolling for you
item.description: Noscroll wants to cure doomscrolling with an AI bot that reads the internet for you.

item.title: Trump’s pick to run US cyber agency CISA asks to drop out
item.description: Sean Plankey has requested to withdraw his name to run the U.S. cybersecurity agency after a tumultuous year of chaotic temporary leadership.

item.title: Google CEO Sundar Pichai says 75% of the company’s code is AI-generated
item.description: As the company goes all in on AI agents, Google engineers are no longer constrained by time or human energy, Google Cloud’s senior director told Fast Company. From software engineers to non-technical staff, Google has urged its employees to fully embrace AI. And it seems like the push to use the tech has resulted in a major productivity leap.

item.title: Meta is downsizing by about 10 percent
item.description: Meta is making another steep cut to its staff, this time to the tune of a 10 percent reduction in its workforce. About 8,000 people will be laid off and about 6,000 open jobs will also be eliminated, according to Bloomberg.In an internal memo from Janelle Gale, Meta's head of human resources, the latest cuts are "part of our continued effort to run the company more efficiently and to allow us to offset the other investments we’re making." Those "other investments" are likely in artificial intelligence. Meta is building its own models and apparently training them on its own staff. Its smart gla...

item.title: Apple’s Next Chapter, SpaceX and Cursor Strike a Deal, and Palantir’s Controversial Manifesto
item.description: In this week’s episode of Uncanny Valley, we talk about Tim Cook’s legacy as CEO at Apple and what his long-rumored departure means for the future of one of the world's biggest companies.

item.title: Microsoft continues a turbulent year with first ever voluntary buyouts
item.description: Certain eligible Microsoft employees will be able to take a voluntary buyout soon, a first for the American tech giant.

item.title: TikTok tests AI Remix option. What it is, how to opt out.
item.description: TikTok is quietly testing a new meme-making AI feature — and opting users in without asking.

item.title: Is that it? IKEA's has a limited-edition merch range to mark its 40th birthday — but it's rather underwhelming
item.description: The Aurtiende range lets you express your IKEA love on your chest, or head — but I think the brand could have put a bit more effort in.

item.title: 'Long term, the only manually driven car will be the Tesla Roadster': Elon Musk says future lineup is going to be almost entirely autonomous
item.description: Tesla's CEO says that the only non-autonomous vehicle in the company's future line-up will be the Roadster.

item.title: The older you get, the harder it is to make friends at work. Here’s why
item.description: And what you can do to curb the biological and social forces at play. After-work drinks are a nice way to bond with colleagues in your 20s and 30s. But, as people get older, different circumstances can necessitate more planning, and new avenues for making friends at work. Take Olga Valadon, 54, whose last corporate role was as chief of staff at Deloitte. ...

item.title: One viral JetBlue blunder has customers convinced it uses surveillance pricing to upcharge on flights
item.description: The airline’s post on X sparked a proposed class action lawsuit over dynamic ticket pricing. JetBlue replies to customers on social media every day, from assuaging their customer service woes to thanking them for choosing their flights. But one seemingly innocuous JetBlue response may have set a class action lawsuit in motion, after customers became convinced that the airline implicated itself in using surveillance pricing....

item.title: ‘We Are Xbox’: read the memo defining Microsoft’s gaming future
item.description: Microsoft's new Xbox CEO Asha Sharma has laid out a new strategy for what she describes as a "return of Xbox." In a joint memo with Xbox Chief Content Officer Matt Booty, Sharma and Booty lay out their vision for the future of Xbox that will be judged on daily active players and the priorities […]
